manifest.name: Select
manifest.description: A customizable and accessible form control for selecting a single option from a dropdown list.

minimal.title: Default
minimal.code:
(
    <Select>
      <SelectTrigger className="nx:w-[180px]" aria-label="Select a fruit">
        <SelectValue placeholder="Select a fruit" />
      </SelectTrigger>
      <SelectContent>
        <SelectItem value="apple">Apple</SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="banana">Banana</SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="orange">Orange</SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="grape">Grape</SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="mango">Mango</SelectItem>
      </SelectContent>
    </Select>
  )

advanced.title: With Default Value
advanced.code:
(
    <Select defaultValue="banana">
      <SelectTrigger className="nx:w-[180px]" aria-label="Select a fruit">
        <SelectValue placeholder="Select a fruit" />
      </SelectTrigger>
      <SelectContent>
        <SelectItem value="apple">Apple</SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="banana">Banana</SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="orange">Orange</SelectItem>
      </SelectContent>
    </Select>
  )

advanced.title: Disabled
advanced.code:
(
    <Select disabled>
      <SelectTrigger className="nx:w-[180px]" aria-label="Disabled select">
        <SelectValue placeholder="Disabled" />
      </SelectTrigger>
      <SelectContent>
        <SelectItem value="apple">Apple</SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="banana">Banana</SelectItem>
      </SelectContent>
    </Select>
  )

advanced.title: With Disabled Items
advanced.code:
(
    <Select>
      <SelectTrigger className="nx:w-[180px]" aria-label="Select an option">
        <SelectValue placeholder="Select option" />
      </SelectTrigger>
      <SelectContent>
        <SelectItem value="option1">Available</SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="option2" disabled>
          Unavailable
        </SelectItem>
        <SelectItem value="option3">Available</SelectItem>
      </SelectContent>
    </Select>
  )

guidance.whenToUse: Use the Select component when users need to choose a single option from a predefined list. It is ideal for form inputs where space is limited and a dropdown menu is preferred over multiple radio buttons, especially when the list of options is moderately long (e.g., 5-15 items).
guidance.whenNotToUse: Do not use Select for very few options (2-3 items); prefer RadioGroup. Avoid when users need to select multiple items, instead use a multi-select or checkbox group. For very long lists requiring search or filtering, consider a Combobox or Autocomplete component to improve user experience.
guidance.accessibility: The Select component built on Radix UI inherently supports robust accessibility. It includes proper ARIA roles and attributes (e.g., "listbox", "aria-haspopup", "aria-expanded", "aria-controls") for screen readers. Keyboard navigation (Tab to focus, Space/Enter to open, Up/Down arrows to navigate options, Enter to select, Escape to close) is fully supported. Ensure the component is always associated with a clear <label> element for optimal screen reader context.

manifest.semanticDescription: The Select component provides a robust and accessible way for users to choose a single value from a predefined list of options. Built on the highly customizable Radix UI primitives, it offers a solid foundation for creating consistent and branded select inputs within a design system. Key features include a customizable trigger button, a scrollable dropdown menu for options, comprehensive keyboard navigation, and inherent accessibility support ensuring it works well with assistive technologies. This component is ideal for scenarios like selecting a country in a registration form, choosing a sorting preference for a data table, or categorizing an item from a fixed set of categories. It effectively manages lists of varying lengths, providing a clean and intuitive user experience while maintaining compliance with accessibility standards.
